Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Decade of Slug 697 upvotes, 68 comments
  2. More than 135 open hardware devices flashable with your own firmware 309 upvotes, 43 comments
  3. Write up of my homebrew CPU build 182 upvotes, 24 comments
  4. Nightingale – open-source karaoke app that works with any song on your computer 358 upvotes, 75 comments
  5. Mistral AI Releases Forge 651 upvotes, 162 comments
  6. A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Show HN: Sub-millisecond VM sandboxes using CoW memory forking 247 upvotes, 62 comments
  8. JPEG Compression 366 upvotes, 97 comments
  9. Meta will shut down VR Horizon Worlds access June 15 27 upvotes, 3 comments
  10. (Media over QUIC) on a Boat 89 upvotes, 20 comments
  11. Pandas Exercises for Data Analysis (Interactive) 108 upvotes, 27 comments
  12. Celebrating Tony Hoare's mark on computer science 90 upvotes, 22 comments
  13. The pleasures of poor product design 206 upvotes, 68 comments
  14. How the Eon Team Produced a Virtual Embodied Fly 64 upvotes, 17 comments
  15. A ngrok-style secure tunnel server written in Rust and Open Source 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  16. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool (just 2 files) to explore the small web 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  18. Federal Cyber Experts Called Microsoft's Cloud "A Pile of Shit", yet Approved It 176 upvotes, 81 comments
  19. Rob Pike's Rules of Programming (1989) 540 upvotes, 290 comments
  20. Nvidia NemoClaw 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Get Shit Done: A meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven dev system 401 upvotes, 220 comments
  22. Python 3.15's JIT is now back on track 449 upvotes, 263 comments
  23. A Tiny Camera Revealed a Hidden Passage in the Great Pyramid 68 upvotes, 31 comments
  24. Death to Scroll Fade 43 upvotes, 18 comments
  25. Restoring the first recording of computer music (2018) 15 upvotes, 4 comments
  26. Machine Payments Protocol (MPP) 36 upvotes, 16 comments
  27. OpenRocket 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Aliens.gov ~ domain registered 17MAR2026 100 upvotes, 77 comments
  30. Ndea (YC W26) is hiring a symbolic RL search guidance lead 0 upvotes, 0 comments
